referenced_section_hint: section 11AF
description: This Prudential Standard is made under section 11AF of the Banking Act 1959.

referenced_section_hint: Definitions
description: Terms defined in APS 001 Definitions appear in bold on first use in this Prudential Standard.

referenced_section_hint: Capital Adequacy: Measurement of Capital — deductions from CET1, T1 and Total Capital
description: Assets required to be deducted from CET1, Tier 1 or Total Capital under APS 111 are excluded from the scope of APS 112.

referenced_section_hint: Securitisation
description: Securitisation exposures subject to APS 120 are excluded from scope, except funding-only or synthetic securitisations where underlying exposures must be included in RWA calculations.

referenced_section_hint: Covered Bonds
description: Liabilities of a covered bond special purpose vehicle to an issuing ADI as specified in APS 121 are excluded from scope.

referenced_section_hint: Capital Adequacy: Market Risk
description: Items subject to capital requirements under APS 116 that do not have counterparty credit risk exposure under APS 180 are excluded from scope.

referenced_section_hint: Capital Adequacy: Counterparty Credit Risk
description: Items subject to APS 116 that do not have counterparty credit risk under APS 180 are excluded from scope. APS 180 also defines netting set and is used for EAD calculation for OTC and exchange-traded derivatives.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment D, paragraph 41 — unrealised fair value losses on liquid assets
description: ADIs with liquid assets at amortised cost may adjust exposures for CET1 deductions to reflect unrealised fair value losses at portfolio level as allowed under paragraph 41 of Attachment D to APS 111.

referenced_section_hint: Liquidity
description: Liquid assets referenced in paragraph 19 are defined by reference to APS 210 Liquidity.

referenced_section_hint: Adjusted current exposure method (CEM) and EAD for OTC and exchange-traded derivatives
description: For significant financial institutions, EAD for OTC and exchange-traded derivative transactions or long-settlement transactions must be determined in accordance with APS 180.

referenced_section_hint: Credit Risk Management — non-performing exposure definition
description: Defaulted exposure is defined by reference to the non-performing exposure definition in APS 220.

referenced_section_hint: netting set definition
description: The term netting set has the meaning given in APS 180.

referenced_section_hint: Associations with Related Entities — related entity definition
description: Related-party exposure is defined by reference to the related entity definition in APS 222.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment E — defaulted exposures
description: Defaulted property exposures must be risk-weighted in accordance with Attachment E rather than Attachment A.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment C — undrawn committed amounts
description: The loan amount for LVR purposes includes any undrawn committed amount as set out in Attachment C to this Prudential Standard.

referenced_section_hint: independent property valuation requirements
description: Property valuations must be appraised independently using prudently conservative criteria consistent with the requirements set out in APS 220.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment D – unsettled and failed transactions
description: Unsettled and failed transactions must be risk-weighted in accordance with Attachment D to this Prudential Standard.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment E – defaulted exposures
description: Defaulted exposures must be risk-weighted in accordance with Attachment E to this Prudential Standard.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment F – external credit rating requirements
description: Where Attachment B requires risk weights based on external credit ratings, ADIs must also comply with the requirements in Attachment F.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment A paragraphs 3 to 7 – standard loan conditions
description: Residential and commercial property exposures in covered bond cover pools must meet the standard loan requirements in paragraphs 3 to 7 of Attachment A.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment B paragraphs 8 to 10 – bank risk weight eligibility for covered bond cover pool
description: Bank assets in covered bond cover pools must qualify for a risk weight of 30 per cent or lower in accordance with paragraphs 8 to 10 of Attachment B.

referenced_section_hint: Table 7 – risk weights for bank exposures
description: Unrated or non-qualifying covered bonds must be treated as bank exposures and risk-weighted using Table 7 of Attachment B.

referenced_section_hint: Table 10 – risk weights for general corporate exposures
description: ADIs must apply Table 10 risk weights to rated general corporate exposures.

referenced_section_hint: Table 11 – risk weights for general corporate exposures (short-term issue-specific credit ratings)
description: ADIs must apply Table 11 risk weights to short-term rated general corporate exposures.

referenced_section_hint: Table 10 – risk weights for general corporate exposures
description: Rated specialised lending exposures with an issue-specific credit rating must be risk-weighted using Table 10.

referenced_section_hint: Deduction of subordinated debt from regulatory capital
description: Subordinated debt not required to be deducted from regulatory capital under APS 111 must be risk-weighted at 150%.

referenced_section_hint: Deduction of equity exposures from Regulatory Capital; deduction threshold for banking or insurance subsidiaries at Level 1 (Attachment D to APS 111)
description: Equity exposures not required to be deducted from Regulatory Capital under APS 111 must be risk-weighted at 250% or 400%; the risk-weighting of equity in banking or insurance subsidiaries at Level 1 is subject to the deduction threshold in Attachment D to APS 111.

referenced_section_hint: Attachments A, B and D – risk-weighting requirements
description: After applying CCFs, committed but undrawn amounts must be risk-weighted according to the requirements in Attachments A, B and D to APS 112.

referenced_section_hint: Risk weights based on counterparty type
description: ADIs must apply the relevant risk weight based on the counterparty as detailed in Attachment B when risk-weighting equity exposures.

referenced_section_hint: Criteria for recognising guarantees for risk-weighting purposes
description: Where a rated entity has guaranteed another entity's exposure, the guarantee may be recognised for risk-weighting purposes if the criteria in Attachment I are satisfied.

referenced_section_hint: Risk weights for counterparties including banks and sovereigns
description: Attachment G references Attachment B for determining risk weights applicable to third-party banks holding cash collateral and for identifying sovereign securities eligible for zero per cent risk weight.

referenced_section_hint: Definition of senior securitisation exposures and resecuritisation exposures
description: Attachment G references APS 120 for the definitions of senior securitisation exposures eligible as collateral and resecuritisation exposures which are excluded from eligible collateral.

referenced_section_hint: Adjusted CEM for OTC derivatives
description: For OTC derivatives to which the ADI applies the adjusted CEM, the EAD calculation under the comprehensive approach references Attachment E to APS 180.

referenced_section_hint: Definition of qualifying central counterparties
description: Attachment G references APS 180 for the definition of qualifying central counterparties, which are listed as core market participants eligible for zero per cent risk weight on qualifying SFTs.

referenced_section_hint: Criteria for credit derivatives — cash-funded credit-linked notes
description: Cash-funded credit-linked notes issued by the ADI against banking book exposures that fulfil the criteria in Attachment J may be treated as cash collateralised transactions under the simple approach.

referenced_section_hint: Eligible bilateral netting agreement requirements
description: SFT netting treatment under the comprehensive approach requires the netting agreement to meet the requirements detailed in Attachment H to this Prudential Standard.

referenced_section_hint: Simple or comprehensive approach for RWA calculation; eligible collateral criteria for banking book
description: On-balance sheet netting for RWA calculation must apply the simple or comprehensive approach as set out in Attachment G; internal banking/trading book netting requires collateralised instruments to comply with eligible collateral criteria in Attachment G.

referenced_section_hint: Deduction from Common Equity Tier 1 Capital for materiality threshold guarantees
description: Where a guarantee provides for a materiality threshold on payments, it must be deducted from Common Equity Tier 1 Capital in accordance with APS 111.

referenced_section_hint: Definition of securitisation exposure
description: Securitisation exposure in Table 23 has the meaning given in APS 120.

referenced_section_hint: paragraph 32 of Attachment G
description: Currency mismatch haircut for guarantees must be adjusted for actual revaluation frequency in accordance with paragraph 32 of Attachment G to APS 112.

referenced_section_hint: synthetic securitisation requirements
description: Where an ADI buys credit protection through a credit derivative forming part of a synthetic securitisation, the requirements of APS 120 apply.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment G – cash-collateralised transactions
description: Cash-funded credit linked notes satisfying minimum credit derivative requirements may be treated as cash-collateralised transactions under Attachment G to APS 112.

referenced_section_hint: CET1 deduction for materiality thresholds
description: Credit derivative materiality thresholds equivalent to retained first loss positions must be deducted from Common Equity Tier 1 Capital in accordance with APS 111.

referenced_section_hint: paragraph 32 of Attachment G
description: Currency mismatch haircut for credit derivatives must be adjusted for actual revaluation frequency in accordance with paragraph 32 of Attachment G to APS 112.

referenced_section_hint: synthetic securitisation – tranched cover
description: Where partial coverage by a credit derivative involves a difference in seniority between protected and unprotected portions, the arrangement is a synthetic securitisation subject to APS 120.
